description_long: This is a metallic adjustable wrench with a robust, utilitarian design, commonly used for gripping and turning nuts and bolts. The wrench features a long, straight handle with a small circular hole at the end, likely for hanging or storage. The head of the wrench is angled and houses a movable jaw, which can be adjusted using a knurled worm screw mechanism visible on the side. The surface of the wrench appears to have a matte, slightly rough finish, with a dark gray coloration and subtle textural variations suggesting wear or industrial use. The jaws are flat and parallel, designed to securely grip fasteners of varying sizes. No visible branding or decorative elements are present, emphasizing its functional, workmanlike character.
description: A dark gray, adjustable metal wrench with a long handle and a movable jaw, featuring a utilitarian, slightly worn finish. The worm screw mechanism is visible on the head for adjusting the jaw width.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full face of the wrench, including the open adjustable jaw and the worm screw mechanism.
description_view_1: Side profile view, revealing the thinness of the wrench and the protrusion of the jaw adjustment mechanism.
description_view_2: Rear view, similar to the front but with the jaw opening facing the opposite direction and less detail on the adjustment mechanism.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile, again showing the thin edge and the adjustment mechanism's outline.
